reason: Response A has the terse, declarative prose style characteristic of Claude Haiku: short punchy sentences, no hedging, and observations stacked without elaborate connective tissue ("Roll a circle and it keeps going. Spin it and nothing changes."). Response B reads like a structured essay with topic sentences, transitional phrases, and organized argument categories (symmetry, nature, math, motion) — a more verbose, performative style that feels like an imitation trying to check all the boxes rather than Haiku's naturally economical voice.
